news.title: World Cup, the highest-scoring finals ⚽️
news.lede: World Cup finals are often tight, cagey matches where every mistake can prove very costly. Yet several of them have produced particularly spectacular, high-scoring encounters. Just hours before the Spain-Argentina showdown, here’s a look back at the highest-scoring finals in World Cup history. Kick-off for the Spain–Argentina final is this Sunday at 9pm.

news.title: When is next World Cup? FIFA Women's World Cup returns next year
news.lede: The 2026 FIFA World Cup is wrapping up with a Spain-Argentina final on July 19. It's the women's edition next year, as the 2027 FIFA Women's World Cup returns to the Southern Hemisphere. Fourteen countries have already qualified for the 2027 tournament. The remaining 18 slots will be filled before the official draw is announced later this year.

news.title: Tuchel gives absurd reason for not playing Saka in World Cup semi final
news.lede: When Bukayo Saka didn’t start England’s semi-final against Argentina, most assumed it was due to his fitness and the Achilles issue that had dogged him for months. But Thomas Tuchel has now revealed he simply picked Morgan Rogers over Saka because he had a “feeling”. Nobody could understand why Saka did not get a single minute against Argentina, with the Arsenal man admitting after he scored a hat-trick against France in the third-place play-off that he was fit and had wanted to play more minutes. “Of course, I would love to have played more,” Saka told the BBC after the game.

news.title: Are money and soft power draining World Cup football of its magic? | Richard Partington
news.lede: Fifa’s orchestration of incompatible or frankly absurd corporate sponsorship suggests the balance may have tipped too far. No more hydration breaks, no more obligatory pans of the TV cameras to Hollywood A-listers, or dread of the crushing inevitability of English disappointment. The Fifa World Cup 2026 is at an end, after another month of planet-straddling drama laced with significance and symbolism, both on and off the pitch. Before the kick-off between Spain and Argentina on Sunday evening, the result is already clear: football’s greatest prize is more than a sporting event; it is a geopolitically charged economic juggernaut.

news.title: England 2026 World Cup squad: player-by-player ratings
news.lede: After the team’s third-placed finish, we rate the 24 squad members who made an appearance at the tournament. Jordan Pickford Could he have done better with Enzo Fernández’s equaliser for Argentina? Jude Bellingham certainly thought so. But the man who has made more World Cup finals appearances than any other Englishman made some crucial saves, including a brilliant stop from Nicolas González in the semi-final.

news.title: Is the FIFA World Cup trophy made of gold? Everything to know
news.lede: One of the biggest flexes an athlete can display is hoisting up a championship trophy, parading around and sharing the moment with their family, friends and fans. And it doesn't get any better than the FIFA World Cup trophy. The World Cup trophy is one of the most luxurious, high-quality, opulent title trophies in all of sports. It's the only championship trophy that is made of real, pure gold.

news.title: Scaloni enters exclusive World Cup managers club
news.lede: Lionel Scaloni has written another chapter in Argentina's football history after guiding La Albiceleste to a second consecutive FIFA World Cup final with a dramatic 2-1 comeback victory over England. After Anthony Gordon handed the Three Lions the lead early in the second half, Argentina responded through Enzo Fernández before Lautaro Martínez struck the winner to send the defending champions into Sunday's showpiece. The result sees Scaloni become just the seventh manager to reach two World Cup finals and only the fifth to do so in successive tournaments. Having led Argentina to World Cup glory in Qatar in 2022, the 48-year-old now has the chance to emulate Italy legend Vittorio Pozzo as the only coaches to win back-to-back World Cup titles.

news.title: Have Your Say: Will Italy fans support Spain or Argentina in 2026 World Cup Final?
news.lede: Spain and Argentina meet in the World Cup Final tonight, and there will be unpleasant implications for Italian football regardless of the outcome. So, who will you support? Spain and Argentina meet in New York for the 2026 World Cup Final tonight. The only two Serie A representatives remaining are both Argentines, Lautaro Martinez and Nico Paz, and neither is expected to start.
